title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Bake Matrix Outputs
summary: A CLI release must support linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 without silently shipping an emulated, untested, or single-platform image. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker bake matrix outputs increment in multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define inherited Bake targets, variables, matrices, groups, contexts, cache, attestations, outputs, and policy while preventing accidental pushes.
Use BUILDPLATFORM, TARGETPLATFORM, TARGETOS, TARGETARCH, and stage pinning without accidentally executing target binaries on the builder.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Define inherited Bake targets, variables, matrices, groups, contexts, cache, attestations, outputs, and policy while preventing accidental pushes. A defensible solution applies Docker Bake Matrix Outputs, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-4
    artifact: docker-bake.hcl
    decision: worked docker multiplatform buildx bake 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      target "release" {
      platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
      attest = ["type=provenance", "type=sbom"]
      output = ["type=registry"]
      }
    verify: docker buildx bake --print release && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate
    failure: Reject architecture emulation as native test evidence, local image load for a multi-platform index, or platform-dependent inputs without declarations.
    repair: Inspect builder capabilities, make target platform explicit, test each artifact, publish the index, and verify per-platform digest selection.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: docker buildx bake always builds one target and loads it into the local image store. This fails the Docker Bake Matrix Outputs contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
